novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2 | Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Misses the closer Cattaneo-Feng-Palomba-Titiunik 2025 scpi/JSS comparator, which implements misspecification-aware prediction intervals across lasso/OLS/ridge/L1-L2 synthetic-control weights; tier=letter below novelty_target=field unless the proposal extends the fixed-oracle interval into an implementable reporting rule or generic-class comparison against this workflow.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Cattaneo-Feng-Palomba-Titiunik 2025 scpi as a load-bearing comparator and state exactly why deterministic sharp partial-ID radii are not covered by their misspecification-aware prediction intervals.

Upgrade Conjecture 1 from a geometric separation note to a field-level diagnostic by proving an open-set result for weights produced by named procedures such as OLS/lasso/ridge/augmented SC, not arbitrary affine pairs.
Add a corollary converting the radius formula into a dominance rule over pre-fit-equivalent specifications and show how it changes MASC or scpi-style reporting tables.

recommended_next_step: Revise §5 and the §8 novelty blocks around the 2025 scpi comparator, then strengthen the flagship from arbitrary fixed weights to named synthetic-control workflows so the tier can plausibly reach field.
